2606.15734.title: Retrievable Gradients: Continual Post-Training Without Cumulative Weight Drift
2606.15734.paper_summary: Continual post-training enables models to absorb emerging knowledge after deployment, but repeatedly updating shared parameters can accumulate weight drift, potentially causing catastrophic forgetting and degrading general capabilities. Retrieval-augmented generation avoids such parameter drift, yet often lacks the depth of parametric knowledge integration. In this paper, we propose ReGrad (Retrievable Gradients), a new paradigm that treats gradients as retrievable units of knowledge. ReGrad pre-computes document-specific gradients offline, stores them in an indexed Gradient Bank, and retrieves only query-relevant gradients at inference time for temporary weight adaptation. However, raw language-modeling gradients are optimized for token-level document reconstruction rather than for query-driven knowledge use. We therefore introduce a bi-level meta-learning objective that reshapes document-derived gradients into generalizable adaptation signals for downstream tasks. Experiments across general and domain-specific settings show that \textsc{ReGrad} outperforms CPT and RAG baselines, enabling scalable and reversible parametric knowledge injection without accumulating weight drift.
2606.15734.paper_summary_zh: 持續的後訓練使得模型能在部署後吸收新出現的知識，但反覆更新共享參數可能會導致權重漂移，潛在地造成災難性的遺忘並降低一般能力。檢索增強生成避免了這種參數漂移，但通常缺乏參數知識整合的深度。在本文中，我們提出了 ReGrad（可檢索梯度），這是一種將梯度視為可檢索知識單元的新範式。ReGrad 在離線時預計算特定於文檔的梯度，將其存儲在索引的梯度庫中，並在推理時僅檢索與查詢相關的梯度以進行臨時權重適應。然而，原始的語言建模梯度是針對令牌級文檔重建進行優化的，而不是針對查詢驅動的知識使用。因此，我們引入了一個雙層元學習目標，將文檔衍生的梯度重塑為可泛化的適應信號，以用於下游任務。在一般和特定領域的實驗中顯示，\textsc{ReGrad} 超越了 CPT 和 RAG 基準，實現了可擴展和可逆的參數知識注入，而不會累積權重漂移。
2606.15734.author: Weihang Su et.al.
2606.15734.authors: Weihang Su, Jiacheng Kang, Jingyan Xu, Qingyao Ai, Jianming Long, Hanwen Zhang, Bangde Du, Xinyuan Cao, Min Zhang, Yiqun Liu
